frontiers.title: Sandy Chaos Proof Frontier V1
frontiers.objective: Force the major Sandy Chaos surfaces through a common proof-path ladder, keep one ranked frontier, and point the next proof move at the strongest discriminating candidate.
frontiers.currentState: `/home/ian/projects/sandy-chaos/docs/proof-path-ladder.md` defines the five-level ladder and `/home/ian/projects/sandy-chaos/plans/today_frontier_2026-04-06.md` records the compression pass that selected `SC-CONCEPT-0006` symbolic-maps-and-narrative-invariants as the active proof surface. Since that selection, the lane has advanced beyond pure framing: `plans/symbolic_maps_level4_benchmark_v0.md` defines the first bounded Level-4 benchmark, `plans/symbolic_maps_level4/comparison_note_v0.md` records a first three-artifact comparison pass, and `plans/symbolic_maps_level4_scoring_pass_v0.md` now records an explicit score pass that confirmed partial support for Arm A. The live pressure is no longer to invent or score the benchmark; it is to run one more bounded benchmark pass and see whether the same advantage survives a slightly more heterogeneous set before any schema hardening.
frontiers.nextAction: Run one more bounded benchmark pass for `SC-CONCEPT-0006` by extending the current artifact set slightly and checking whether Arm A retains its advantage in consistency, structural coverage, and downstream reuse. Do not move into validator/schema hardening unless that next bounded pass reveals one precise structural slot that clearly deserves it.
frontiers.selectionReason: Current active Ygg baton in assistant-home.

frontiers.title: Idea Capture Rhythm V1
frontiers.objective: Design and operationalize an idea-capture + brainstorming protocol that maps Sandy Chaos tempo/rhythm doctrine into daily human-machine workflow and communication channels.
frontiers.currentState: Added a compact lane-first operating note at config/IDEA_CAPTURE_RHYTHM_LANE_NOTE.md to replace overreliance on the larger protocol draft. The lane is now framed as a lightweight rhythm for catching useful sparks without killing momentum.
frontiers.nextAction: Use the three cues in live work, notice which feel natural vs annoying, and capture 2-3 real fragments before deciding what should harden.

frontiers.title: Sandy Chaos Alignment Constraints V1
frontiers.objective: Translate the strongest Sandy Chaos ideals into concrete Ygg design constraints so Ygg evolves in sync with the host project rather than drifting into a merely clever control shell.
frontiers.currentState:
Completed the first compact constraint packet in `config/SANDY_CHAOS_ALIGNMENT_CONSTRAINT_PACKET_V1.md`, seeded the first advisory `state/concept-spine.json` surface, wired the `work` front door to consume baton state plus `state/active-work.json` plus `state/concept-spine.json`, and implemented Dispatch Phase 2a from `config/YGG_DISPATCH_PHASE2A_MINISPEC.md`. The limiting reagent then shifted from dispatch polish to proof-governance compression, so the next pass produced a durable Sandy Chaos proof-path ladder, a ranked frontier note, a new Sandy Chaos spine concept (`SC-CONCEPT-0008`), and a pressure event that selected the next proof surface.

Completion note: alignment constraints work successfully handed off into the Sandy Chaos proof-frontier lane.
frontiers.nextAction: Resume `ygg-dev--sandy-chaos-proof-frontier-v1` for the next active move: define one Level-4 discriminating benchmark for `SC-CONCEPT-0006` symbolic-maps-and-narrative-invariants.
